qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded request body parsing enables denial of service via memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding references the Agent endpoint as one affected request-body consumer, but its root cause is missing request body size limits and memory exhaustion, not the advisory's missing per-app authorization check or read-only master-key restriction in POST /apps/:appId/agent.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is missing authorization for POST /apps/:appId/agent, including cross-app access by authenticated users and improper full master-key use for read-only users when agent configuration is enabled. The only manifest-listed qualifying finding covers unrelated request body memory exhaustion.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-cvwj-6c9h-jg6v describes missing per-app authorization and read-only master-key restrictions in Parse Dashboard's Agent endpoint.
unbounded_request_body_dos/vulnerability_report.json describes unbounded body parsing and memory exhaustion, not an authorization or master-key selection flaw.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so no Tier1, Tier2, or Tier3 classification applies.
